stub: She is a Labour MP who comes from a white middle class background.
constituency: She is from a constituency that is not your constituency.
personalization: She tends to vote in accordance with the wishes of her constituents, even when that conflicts with the position of her party.
substantive: She opposes increased funding for the NHS.
justification: In political debates, she regularly uses arguments that appeal to society as a whole.
responsiveness: Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said: “I did not go into politics to win elections, but to make this country a better place.”
text: She is a Labour MP who comes from a white middle class background. She is from a constituency that is not your constituency. She tends to vote in accordance with the wishes of her constituents, even when that conflicts with the position of her party. She opposes increased funding for the NHS. In political debates, she regularly uses arguments that appeal to society as a whole. Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said: “I did not go into politics to win elections, but to make this country a better place.”
